title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · Cluster Case: K8s Service Types Exposure
summary: A frontend loses backends as Pods rotate because it pins Pod IPs; a Service selector overlaps a canary and traffic policies change source and locality behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s service types exposure increment in service discovery and endpoint evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints. A defensible solution applies K8s Service Types Exposure, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to service discovery and endpoint evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-5
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s services endpoints dns 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: LoadBalancer replaces ClusterIP and works identically without a provider implementation. This fails the K8s Service Types Exposure contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
